chunk_summary: This section focuses on monitoring anthropometric data and involving dietitians in managing therapeutic elimination diets for food allergies, with emphasis on cow’s milk protein allergy and eosinophilic esophagitis in children.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of anthropometric monitoring in elimination diet management.
Learn the importance of dietitian referrals for nutrition-safe elimination diets.
Identify key guidelines for managing cow’s milk protein allergy and eosinophilic esophagitis.
Recognize the diagnostic criteria for food protein-induced enterocolitis syndrome (FPIES).
key_facts: Anthropometric monitoring is critical to ensure proper growth and nutritional status during elimination diets.
Cow’s milk protein allergy is a common condition requiring elimination diets, as per Indian Academy of Pediatrics guidelines.
Eosinophilic esophagitis management in children involves dietary interventions, as outlined by ESPGHAN (2024).
FPIES diagnosis and management guidelines were established by the American Academy of Allergy, Asthma & Immunology in 2017.
Dietitian involvement ensures balanced nutrition and prevents deficiencies during elimination diets.
reference_quote: Anthropometric monitoring and dietitian involvement are essential for managing cow’s milk protein allergy and eosinophilic esophagitis in children (ESPGHAN, 2024).
document_key_topics: Role of dietitians in food allergy management
